calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-05-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Case And For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Yosef Dangor And Maurice Wainer In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-11-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andre Lelievre of the law offices of Norther California Collection Service, Inc. (916-929-0130) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Maurice Wainer of the law offices of Snipper Wainer & Markoff (310-550-5700) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANTS, ONLY $1 $2 $3 FASHION, GATEWAY STAFFING SERVICES INC., DRLY INC. and TOP TOP CLOTHING INC.'S Motion To Transfer Case And For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs is GRANTED. This case is transferred to the Central District of the Los Angeles Superior Court. Plaintiff Northern California Collection Service, Inc. must pay all costs of transfer. NCCS' counsel Steven Cribb and Andre LeLievre are required to pay to defendants $4719 in fees and costs no later than January 12, 2018. There are three independent and sufficient reasons why venue does not lie in San Francisco for this case. First, NCCS is bound by its venue allegations in the prior case it filed in Los Angeles Superior Court and may not invoke the venue of this court by making contrary venue allegations at least where, as here, no credible explanation has been provided for the inconsistent venue allegations. Second, NCCS is estopped from filing another lawsuit in a different county based on the same alleged debt after litigating its earlier lawsuit in Los Angeles County for over a year and dismissing that case shortly before trial. It is contrary to public policy, demeaning to the judicial system and violative of the most basic concepts of judicial economy for a plaintiff to extensively litigate a case in one county and then just before trial dismiss the case only to re-file it in another county. Third, NCCS has provided insufficient evidentiary support for its assertion that venue lies in this county per CCP 395.5. Because filing this lawsuit in San Francisco was not made in good faith, this court exercises its discretion to award reasonable fees and costs to defendants. NCCS does not contest that defendants incurred $4719 in fees and costs in bringing this motion, so that is the amount awarded. Transfer and payment of fees and costs (sanctions) are due within 30 days. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)
